SEARCH
Package com.societegenerale.failover.store

Class FailoverStoreAsync<T>

java.lang.Object
com.societegenerale.failover.store.FailoverStoreAsync<T>
Type Parameters:
T - the type of the payload
All Implemented Interfaces:
FailoverStore<T>
public class FailoverStoreAsync<T> extends Object implements FailoverStore<T>
FailoverStore decorator that offloads write operations (store(com.societegenerale.failover.core.payload.ReferentialPayload<T>), delete(com.societegenerale.failover.core.payload.ReferentialPayload<T>), cleanByExpiry(java.time.Instant)) to a TaskExecutor, keeping the calling thread unblocked. find(java.lang.String, java.lang.String) remains synchronous because the caller needs the result.

Threading contract

Each write method captures only the method arguments in its executor lambda — never any ThreadLocal values (such as a tenant context or security context). This is intentional:

  • Tenant routing (when multitenant) is performed by the outermost wrapper (MultiTenantFailoverStore) on the calling thread, before this class is reached. By the time store(com.societegenerale.failover.core.payload.ReferentialPayload<T>) is invoked, this.failoverStore is already scoped to the correct tenant — no re-resolution is needed inside the executor.
  • ThreadLocal values are not propagated to executor threads by default in Java. Relying on them inside the lambda would cause silent, hard-to-debug failures on pooled threads. The design avoids this by ensuring all context-sensitive routing happens before the thread boundary.

The TaskExecutor is injected at construction time so this decorator works correctly whether the instance is a Spring-managed bean or created programmatically inside a factory (e.g. one per tenant in multitenant mode) — unlike Spring's @Async annotation, which silently degrades to synchronous execution for non-Spring-managed instances.

Author:
Anand Manissery

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    FailoverStoreAsync(FailoverStore<T> failoverStore, org.springframework.core.task.TaskExecutor executor)
    Creates an async decorator that only logs failures (no metric emission).
    FailoverStoreAsync(FailoverStore<T> failoverStore, org.springframework.core.task.TaskExecutor executor, @Nullable ObservablePublisher observablePublisher)
    Creates an async decorator that additionally reports executor-side failures to the given publisher.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    cleanByExpiry(Instant expiry)
    Submits the cleanup operation to the executor.
    void
    delete(ReferentialPayload<T> referentialPayload)
    Submits the delete operation to the executor.
    Optional<ReferentialPayload<T>>
    find(String name, String key)
    Executes find synchronously on the calling thread — the result is needed immediately.
    List<ReferentialPayload<T>>
    findAll(String name)
    Executes findAll synchronously on the calling thread — the result is needed immediately, so no executor boundary is crossed (same rationale as find(java.lang.String, java.lang.String)).
    void
    store(ReferentialPayload<T> referentialPayload)
    Submits the store operation to the executor.

  • Constructor Details

    • FailoverStoreAsync

      public FailoverStoreAsync(FailoverStore<T> failoverStore, org.springframework.core.task.TaskExecutor executor)
      Creates an async decorator that only logs failures (no metric emission).
      Parameters:
      failoverStore - the delegate store
      executor - the executor that runs write operations off the calling thread

    • FailoverStoreAsync

      public FailoverStoreAsync(FailoverStore<T> failoverStore, org.springframework.core.task.TaskExecutor executor, @Nullable ObservablePublisher observablePublisher)
      Creates an async decorator that additionally reports executor-side failures to the given publisher.
      Parameters:
      failoverStore - the delegate store
      executor - the executor that runs write operations off the calling thread
      observablePublisher - sink notified on async failure; null disables metric emission

  • Method Details

    • store

      public void store(ReferentialPayload<T> referentialPayload)
      Submits the store operation to the executor.

      referentialPayload is captured by reference in the lambda — safe because ReferentialPayload is treated as immutable after being passed here. No ThreadLocal values are read inside the lambda.

      Specified by:
      store in interface FailoverStore<T>
      Parameters:
      referentialPayload - the payload to store; must not be null

    • delete

      public void delete(ReferentialPayload<T> referentialPayload)
      Submits the delete operation to the executor. No ThreadLocal values are read inside the lambda.
      Specified by:
      delete in interface FailoverStore<T>
      Parameters:
      referentialPayload - the payload to remove; must not be null

    • find

      public Optional<ReferentialPayload<T>> find(String name, String key)
      Executes find synchronously on the calling thread — the result is needed immediately. No thread boundary is crossed.
      Specified by:
      find in interface FailoverStore<T>
      Parameters:
      name - the referential name (e.g. the entity or endpoint identifier)
      key - the unique key within that referential
      Returns:
      an Optional containing the stored payload, or empty if not found

    • findAll

      public List<ReferentialPayload<T>> findAll(String name) throws FailoverStoreException
      Executes findAll synchronously on the calling thread — the result is needed immediately, so no executor boundary is crossed (same rationale as find(java.lang.String, java.lang.String)).
      Specified by:
      findAll in interface FailoverStore<T>
      Parameters:
      name - the referential name
      Returns:
      all matching payloads, or an empty list if none exist
      Throws:
      FailoverStoreException - if the underlying lookup operation fails

    • cleanByExpiry

      public void cleanByExpiry(Instant expiry)
      Submits the cleanup operation to the executor. No ThreadLocal values are read inside the lambda.
      Specified by:
      cleanByExpiry in interface FailoverStore<T>
      Parameters:
      expiry - the cutoff instant; entries whose expireOn is before this value are removed
